refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated capability to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints), record derived requirements with traceability to source inputs, and maintain a managed baseline history including calibration updates and performance target revisions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional concerns: derivation (analysis/synthesis), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into analyzable sub-functions while maintaining traceability to worksite inputs and baseline management as acceptance criteria. High safety criticality demands explicit linkage to safety constraints in derivation logic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall demonstrate: (1) a documented derivation method that maps worksite inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) to performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability); (2) a recorded baseline with full traceability to source inputs; (3) a change history showing at least two calibration/update cycles with audit trail; (4) confirmation that safety-critical performance targets are flagged and reviewed by Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints) using a documented method.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation assumptions.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, including calibration updates, revisions, and audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure safety-critical performance requirements are identified, validated by Safety Manager, and linked to worksite safety plan.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance requirement database with derivation rules engine, baseline versioning, and audit logging; integrated with worksite mission input capture and safety constraint mapping.
DP-B: Distributed spreadsheet-based templates with manual derivation, local file versioning, and email-based change notification.
DP-C: Hybrid approach: lightweight derivation module with rule-based performance target calculation, coupled to a centralized baseline repository with change control and traceability views.
DP-D: Manual derivation by Site Manager with documented worksheets, stored in project file system with periodic backup and manual audit trail.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid approach with a rule-based derivation module (logic for mapping worksite inputs to performance targets) coupled to a centralized baseline repository (versioning, audit trail, traceability views). This balances operational flexibility (local rule customization) with governance (centralized baseline control and audit compliance).

potential_sources: Derivation module must receive worksite mission context (site conditions, task type, safety constraints) as inputs; these are external to the performance requirement system and must be captured via interface.
Baseline repository must integrate with Safety Services to flag and validate safety-critical targets; this is a controlled dependency, not a coupling violation.
Change control logic in repository is independent of derivation logic; separation of concerns is maintained.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits low coupling: derivation rules are decoupled from baseline storage via a clear interface (derived targets -> repository). External dependencies (worksite inputs, safety validation) are explicit and manageable. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints) is available as structured input at the time of requirement derivation.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Manager is available to validate safety-critical performance targets within acceptable turnaround time.
RISK: If derivation rules are not well-documented or validated, derived targets may be incorrect or inconsistent across projects.
RISK: If baseline repository does not enforce strict change control, audit trail integrity may be compromised.
RISK: Integration with Safety Services may introduce latency in requirement approval if safety validation is not streamlined.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the source and format of worksite mission context inputs (e.g., structured data from site survey, operator input form, or external system). Define the rule set for derivation (e.g., lookup tables, algorithms, or expert system) and establish a validation process for rules before deployment. Specify the interface contract between derivation module and baseline repository (e.g., JSON schema for derived targets, versioning protocol).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides the overarching capability to manage performance requirements. Within this scope, the performance requirement derivation and baseline management capability is realized as a logical subsystem (Performance Requirement Management subsystem) that may be implemented as a software service, database, and associated tools.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, operational policies) from Site Manager or external mission planning system. This input drives performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends safety-critical performance targets to Safety Services for validation and receives safety constraints/approvals. Ensures performance requirements are aligned with worksite safety plan.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance targets and baseline history to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems for operational use and monitoring. Also receives actual performance data for comparison against targets and calibration updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for baseline repository access, change notifications, and audit trail synchronization across distributed sites and stakeholders.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary and is the natural home for performance requirement management. This is a core system capability that must be integrated across all subsystems. High containment score reflects that the entire capability (derivation, recording, management) logically belongs at the system level. Low diffusion score indicates minimal risk of scattering this capability across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks and reporting telemetry. It should consume performance targets (from NGHE Solution) but should not own the derivation or baseline management logic. Assigning this capability to Vehicle would scatter requirement management across the platform and create tight coupling between vehicle firmware and requirement logic. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and monitoring. It should consume performance targets and report actual performance, but should not own derivation or baseline management. Assigning this capability to Teleoperation would create a dependency on tele-operator availability for requirement management and scatter logic across the system. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services is responsible for safety validation and alerts. It should validate safety-critical performance targets and provide safety constraints, but should not own the overall requirement derivation or baseline management. Assigning this capability to Safety Services would conflate safety validation (a Safety Services responsibility) with requirement lifecycle management (a system-level responsibility). High diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Performance requirement derivation and baseline management is a system-level capability that must be owned by the NGHE Solution module. This capability integrates inputs from worksite mission context (Tasking), safety constraints (SafetyAlert), and actual performance data (Telemetry), and distributes derived targets to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. Centralizing this capability in NGHE Solution ensures consistency, traceability, and governance across all subsystems and stakeholders.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module becomes too large or monolithic, it may be difficult to maintain and evolve. Mitigation: Decompose NGHE Solution into logical subsystems (e.g., Performance Requirement Management, Mission Planning, Safety Coordination) with clear interfaces.

RISK: If performance requirement management is tightly coupled to specific Vehicle or Teleoperation implementations, it may not be reusable across different platforms. Mitigation: Define DP-C (hybrid derivation + repository) with platform-agnostic interfaces and rule sets.

RISK: If baseline repository is not properly secured and access-controlled, unauthorized changes to performance targets could compromise safety or performance. Mitigation: Implement role-based access control (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality) and audit logging in the baseline repository.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish a clear interface contract between NGHE Solution (Performance Requirement Management subsystem) and Vehicle/Teleoperation subsystems for consumption of performance targets. Define the data schema for performance targets (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) and the protocol for baseline updates. Specify the role-based access control and audit logging requirements for the baseline repository. Clarify the integration points with Safety Services for safety-critical target validation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid derivation + repository) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) rule-based derivation module maps worksite inputs to performance targets; (2) centralized repository records targets with traceability; (3) versioning and audit logging support baseline management; (4) integration with Safety Services enables safety-critical target validation. No gaps or misalignments detected. DP-C is feasible, testable, and aligns with acceptance criteria (documented performance targets, traceability, baseline history).

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): If performance requirement management is implemented as a monolithic software service within NGHE Solution, it may be difficult to evolve or replace individual components (e.g., derivation rules, baseline repository). Mitigation: Use modular architecture (e.g., microservices, plugin-based rules engine) to enable independent evolution.

DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): If the baseline repository is implemented as a centralized database, it may become a single point of failure. Mitigation: Implement redundancy, backup, and disaster recovery procedures.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for this capability. The module containment analysis shows 95% containment score for NGHE Solution vs. 20-30% for Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services. Performance requirement management is a system-level concern that must be integrated across all subsystems. No diffusion to other modules is recommended. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined and manageable.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible and traceable. The refined FR specifies four sub-FRs with clear verification criteria: (1) documented derivation method with worksite input mapping; (2) recorded baseline with traceability; (3) change history with audit trail; (4) safety-critical target identification and validation. Each sub-FR can be verified through document review, system testing, and audit trail inspection. Acceptance criteria (documented performance targets, traceability, baseline history) are directly verifiable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear. CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution (system-level). Sub-FRs are allocated to logical subsystems within NGHE Solution: CR-NGHE-005.1 (derivation module), CR-NGHE-005.2 (baseline repository), CR-NGHE-005.3 (versioning/audit), CR-NGHE-005.4 (Safety Services integration). Each sub-FR has a clear owner and interface contract.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Clarify the source and format of worksite mission context inputs (Tasking interface). Define a structured data schema (e.g., JSON, XML) for site conditions, task objectives, and safety constraints. This will enable automated derivation and reduce manual interpretation errors.

REVISION 2: Specify the derivation rule set and validation process. Document the algorithms, lookup tables, or expert system rules used to map worksite inputs to performance targets. Establish a change control process for rule updates and a validation procedure (e.g., peer review, historical data validation) before deployment.

REVISION 3: Define the baseline repository interface contract. Specify the data schema for performance targets, versioning protocol, change notification mechanism, and audit trail format. This will enable integration with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems.

REVISION 4: Establish role-based access control and audit logging requirements. Define which stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality, System Engineer) have read/write access to performance targets and baseline history. Specify audit logging requirements (who, what, when, why) for compliance and traceability.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (derivation, recording, management, safety validation) that are logically distinct and require separate design solutions. Each sub-FR has different stakeholders, interfaces, and verification criteria. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will enable detailed design of the derivation module, baseline repository, and Safety Services integration. The FR-DP-IM gates all pass, indicating that the current level is sound and ready for decomposition.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints) using a documented method.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation assumptions.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, including calibration updates, revisions, and audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure safety-critical performance requirements are identified, validated by Safety Manager, and linked to worksite safety plan.

assumptions: Worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints) is available as structured input at the time of requirement derivation.
Safety Manager is available to validate safety-critical performance targets within acceptable turnaround time.
Performance requirement derivation can be automated or semi-automated using rule-based logic (lookup tables, algorithms, or expert system).
Baseline repository can be implemented as a centralized system with version control, audit logging, and role-based access control.
Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems can consume performance targets via a well-defined interface (Telemetry).
Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth and reliability for baseline repository access and change notifications across distributed sites.
open_risks: RISK: Derivation rules may not be well-validated or consistent across projects, leading to incorrect or inconsistent performance targets. Mitigation: Establish a rule validation process and maintain a rule library with version control.
RISK: Baseline repository may become a single point of failure if not properly redundant or backed up. Mitigation: Implement redundancy, backup, and disaster recovery procedures.

RISK: Safety Manager validation may introduce latency in requirement approval if not streamlined. Mitigation: Define expedited approval process for non-critical targets and pre-approved rule sets for common scenarios.

RISK: Integration with Safety Services may reveal conflicts between performance targets and safety constraints. Mitigation: Establish a conflict resolution process and escalation path to Site Manager and Safety Manager.

RISK: Stakeholders (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Maintenance/Quality) may have different expectations for performance targets. Mitigation: Establish clear communication and stakeholder engagement process during requirement derivation.
evidence_needed: Documented derivation method with worksite input mapping (e.g., decision tree, algorithm, rule set).
Baseline repository design specification with data schema, versioning protocol, and audit logging requirements.
Interface contract between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation subsystems for performance target consumption.
Role-based access control and audit logging specification for baseline repository.
Integration specification with Safety Services for safety-critical target validation.
Validation plan for derivation rules (e.g., peer review, historical data validation, pilot testing).
Change control process for performance target updates and baseline revisions.
Traceability matrix linking performance targets to worksite mission context inputs and acceptance criteria.
